title: SQL Repositories, Pools, Transactions, and Request Consistency · Runtime Contract: Transaction Boundary Isolation
summary: Handlers open a new database handle per request, concatenate resource IDs, leak rows, perform multi-step writes outside transactions, and return before commit ambiguity is resolved. Planned artifact: Add and verify the httpsgo transaction boundary isolation increment in context-bound repository and transaction contract. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Define atomic invariants, BeginTx with context and isolation, defer rollback, commit exactly once, and handle PostgreSQL Read Committed changes.
Use dialect parameters, QueryContext, Scan, rows Close and Err, bounded result sets, and error identity without constructing SQL from input.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Go server Predict Transaction Boundary Isolation · Theory Transaction Boundary Isolation
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory team handles case 2294 in a canary service revision draining long-lived streams. Build deterministic Go evidence to classify transaction outcome without retrying an ambiguous commit; then repeat the request with a different method and retain status plus durable-effect evidence. Browser code models decisions only. Listener, TLS, database, proxy, race, load, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates.
